reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary:
reviewer felt that the patch description was redundant and unnecessary, suggesting it should only be in the commit message

Reviewer NeilBrown suggested simplifying the nfs4_init_cp_state() function by removing the cs_type parameter and instead initializing it to zero, which is invalid. He proposed that callers of this function set the type themselves, as stateid types are already checked elsewhere.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown noted that the switch to list_for_each_entry_safe() is unnecessary and prefers using while() loops for clarity, suggesting a specific alternative implementation
reviews.raw_body:
The switch from while() to list_for_each_entry_safe() isn't required for
this change, and isn't justified by the commit message.

I prefer while() is it makes it abundantly clear that the list will be
emptied.
I personally prefer

  while ((cps = list_first_entry_or_null(&stid->sc_cp_list,
				       struct nfs4_cpntf_state,
				       cp_list)) != NULL) {

but I understand that might not be everyone's favourite.

Apart from the comment, the code changes looks correct.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown expressed concerns about the clarity of the patch, specifically pointing out that the barrier around assignment of ->cp_clp is unclear without additional context in the comments, and suggested breaking up the patch into two smaller patches to reduce noise.
reviews.raw_body:
This patch seems to make two changes.
One involves extra refcount on the "copy" structure, which is probably
sensible but hard for me to follow because the patch is so noisy.

The other is barriers around assignment of ->cp_clp which doesn't make
any sense to me at all, and the copious comments don't help with.
A barrier is always between two things.  Maybe we assign NULL to
something before testing something else, or maybe we test something
before an assignment.  The comments should make clear what the something
else is.  They don't (at least not to me).

I would rather two patches with less noise and more useful comments.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch series fixes bugs in the inter-server copy offload code, specifically a publish race condition and use-after-free issues in the cpntf (copy-on-write) state machine.
